TOPIC: Starting_Supervisor_for_First_Time.htm
========================================
Starting PcVue for the first time
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Starting PcVue for the First Time
 
After installation, PcVue is started by clicking on its icon in
 the Start Menu or on the desktop. The first thing that appears is the loading window (the Event Viewer) that displays status and error messages while the various
 software managers are being loaded. Show picture
 

 

 
 
 
 
Once PcVue is running, the Event Viewer is hidden
 by the main window. It can be moved in front of the main
 window at any time (providing the Event Viewer is not minimized) using
 function key F7.
 
The first time you run PcVue, the Event Viewer
 opens in English. This start-up default language can be changed by modifying the Languages settings.
 
Modes
 
If you try to start the software without a hardware protection
 key, a dialog will ask you to insert one. Show picture
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
To run PcVue
 in demonstration/evaluation mode, select Start in demonstration mode.
 
To run the mode appropriate
 to the hardware protection key, insert the key and select Try again.
 
To cancel startup,
 select Exit.
 
 
For details of versions of PcVue, see the Installation book.
 
The Event Viewer will display some more messages about
 loading and creating variables.
 
Creating or selecting a project
 

 
Starting and running a project on a networked disk drive is not supported.
 
 
You can create a new project or select an existing one
 as follows.
 
 
Select the menu Configure.Project.Select Project to open the Select Project dialog.
 
Either select an
 existing project from the list, or type in a new project name and select
 OK.
 Show picture

 
Before a new project
 is created, you are asked to confirm the action. Select OK.
 
 

 
The complete path for the project must not exceed 255 characters.
 
 

 
If you select a project created with an earlier version of PcVue, which uses an HDS database, you may have to migrate the database prior to starting the project. See the topic Overview of database migration for more information.


========================================
TOPIC: Starting_the_DB_Import_Tool.htm
========================================
Starting the Database Import Tool
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Starting the Database Import Tool
 
The Database Import tool is installed as part of the PcVue installation process, but does not require it to be running.
 
 
In the Windows Start menu, select All Programs, then in PcVue's sub-folder select Tools. Database Import.
 
 
Modes
 
The Database Import Tool has two modes of operation.
 
 
Wizard mode. The import is an interactive process using a wizard from which you can select the file location, period, database and table, and filter the records according to variable name. For details, see the book on Wizard Mode.
 
Express mode. The import is not interactive although it does return an error code that can be used if running from a batch file. A configuration file and command line arguments are used to select the file location, period, database and table. The variable names cannot be selected (records for all variables are imported). For details, see the topics Configuration File Format and Command Line Arguments.
 
 
Where the data files are stored
 
In either mode, one of the parameters is the location of the data to be imported. By default, files resulting from the Database Export process are held in a subfolder in PcVue's root folder: \Databases\Export. The structure is created automatically when the data files are first exported to the location. It contains one subfolder corresponding to each table. Normally there are two, one for the trend table and one for the log table. Show picture
 

 
 
 
However, in general the data files can be in any storage location that is accessible for reading by the Database Import tool.


========================================
TOPIC: Startup.htm
========================================
Station startup
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Station Startup
 
The Station Startup settings are displayed from the Application Explorer's main settings dialog. Show picture

 
Automatically displaying the first window
 
Nearly all applications automatically display a window after PcVue has started. The window provides an entry point for the application - a typical use is a menu or a plant overview. To automatically display a window on startup: 
 
 
Tick the First window box. 
 
Select the mimic name from the Mimic drop down list box. 
 
Select or enter a database branch for use with the mimic. 
 
 
Running user (SCADA Basic) programs on startup
 
If your project includes programs written in SCADA Basic you must arrange for them load and run when the project starts. Using the project start-up dialog you can select up to five programs.
 
 
Declarations - The name of the declarations program, which contains common declarations and sub-routines. If you run any SCADA Basic programs, you must also specify a declarations program (commonly called global.svb) See the book on programming for more information. 
 
Programs before initialization - The name of up to two SCADA basic programs that will be run before variables are initialized. 
 
Programs after initialization - The name of up to two SCADA basic programs that will be run after variables are initialized. 
 
 
To enable the running of programs at start-up you must tick the box Run user programs.
 
What is initialization?
 
At the start of PcVue, each variable subscribes to its configured source (internal, equipment, DDE, OPC, LNS, BACnet etc.). The component responsible for that source will, one by one, reply to the variable subscriptions with either a valid value or an invalid status. An invalid status may occur for a variety of reasons, for example a failure in industrial communication. When this process is complete, variable initialization is said to be finished even if one or more of the variables have an invalid status. Therefore, even programs that are started after initialization must be designed in such a way that erroneous operation does not occur if any variables to which they refer are invalid. 
 
Starting communication 
 
To start native communication drivers and DDE exchanges at start-up you must tick the corresponding boxes. Whilst developing a project it is often convenient not to start communication or DDE exchanges automatically in which case they can be started manually from the communication and DDE configuration dialogs.


========================================
TOPIC: Station_advanced_tab.htm
========================================
The station Advanced tab
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The Station Advanced Tab
 
The Station Advanced tab contains properties that, for 
 most applications, will not need to be changed from the default. Show picture

 
 
Availability rate - Determines how the server behaves 
 when it is part of a Real-time or Historical Association. For more information, see 
 the book Using client-server archive units.
 
Gateway - Must be selected on any station that is both client and server. That is, it can behave as a gateway. Only required when using three level network architecture.
Route log- The station routes log and event messages to the client stations. These messages are needed for the station that records log and event information. Only required when using three level network architecture.
 
Configuration backup files - The number of previous versions of configuration files that are kept for this station. Previous versions are stored in the project folder CTEMP\BAK.
 
Remote Desktop Session Host Server - See the book on Deploying PcVue on a RDS host in the Deployment book for information.
Run only in a Remote Desktop Session - If ticked then PcVue will only run this station in a Remote Desktop Session. When starting, if the software detects that it is not in a Remote Desktop Session and that this option is selected, it will display an error message and start-up will abort.
Workspace behavior
Use the same working folder for all Remote Desktop Session hosted clients - The same working (project) folder is used for all Remote Desktop sessions. This is the same behavior as with versions prior to 10.0 SP1.
Use a different working folder for each Remote Desktop Session hosted client - The project folder is duplicated at startup to ensure a different working folder is used for each Remote Desktop session.
 
Network dongle - The configuration required if using a network dongle. A network dongle is one that has several licenses in the same dongle. Typically used where PcVue is running on Windows Server using Remote Desktop Services.
Network dongle role - Tick if the station is to use a network dongle.
Owner or renter - Selection of the station role as a dongle owner or renter.
Owner - The station is a network dongle owner. That is the dongle is physically connected to the host PC running PcVue locally.
Renter - The station is a network dongle renter. That is PcVue on this station will retrieve its license from another station configured as a dongle owner.
Network dongle location - The location of the network dongle license.
Slot number - The slot number (either 1 or 2).
Owner's station name or Association name - The name of the station, or association to which the dongle is physically connected. In the case of an association, two dongles are required with the active server having the role of dongle owner.


========================================
TOPIC: STATION_FILTER.htm
========================================
STATION_FILTER
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
STATION_FILTER
 

 
 

 

 

 See also 

Example
 
Manage the application of population filters to functions and data.
 
WebVue support - Yes.
 

 
Population filtering based on the Source is not supported to filter in log viewers (and log printing) when data are archived using a database archive unit (HDS).
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Syntax
 
 
 

 
 1 
 
 
 CLEAR 
 
 1
 
 
 

 
 2 
 
 
 APPLY 
 
 2, 3
 
 

 
 3 
 
 
 DUMP 
 
 1
 
 
 
 
All Syntaxes
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
Distribution
 
 
 
A numeric code, represented as a bitfield, that defines which functions of PcVue is to be affected:
 
 
Bit 1 - Alarm Viewers and audible alarm.
 
Bit 2 - Log Viewers.
 
Bit 3 - Recording log data on the printer.
 
Bit 4 - Recording log data on the archive units.
 
Bit 5 - Sending values by the operator.
 
Bit 7 - Sending values from the program language.
 
Bit 8 - Acknowledging alarms using the Run macro animation.
 
Bit 9 - Acknowledging alarms using the program language.
 
 
 Type INTEGER.
 

 
Combinations of functions are enabled by setting the corresponding bits in the Distribution field. For example, Distribution = 3 (binary 00000011) activates Bit 1 (Alarm viewer display) and Bit 2 (Log viewer display).
 
 
 
 

 List
 
 
 
A comma-separated list of population names, for example ZONE1,ZONE2,COMMON. Type STR.
 
 
 
 

 
The list is limited to a maximum of 64 names and a total of 1,024 characters. 
 
 
Syntax 1
 
IntVal = STATION_FILTER(Mode, Distribution);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
1
 
 
 
CLEAR
 
 
 
All population filters that have been previously applied for the function corresponding to Distribution are cleared.
 
 
 

 
 
3
 
 
 
DUMP
 
 
 
Display the configuration of Distribution in the results area of the SCADA BASIC Program Management window (you can display this window using F9).
 
 
 

 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
Return: 1 if successful, else 0.
 
 
 
 
Syntax 2
 
IntVal = STATION_FILTER(Mode, Distribution, 
 List);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
2
 
 
 
APPLY
 
 
 
The list of population filters defined in List is applied to the function defined by Distribution. An OR is applied, any item matching one or the other population filter will be accessible to the user.
 
Return: 1 if successful, else 0.
 
 
 
 
Syntax 3
 
IntVal = STATION_FILTER(Mode, Handle);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
Handle
 
 
 
The location of a memory buffer containing a function code and a list of population filters. Type LONG.
 
Memory buffer example:
 
Format: FILTER,0,Distribution,List
 
 
 
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
2
 
 
 
APPLY
 
 
 
The list of population filters defined in Handle is applied to the function defined in Handle. An OR is applied, any item matching one or the other population filter will be accessible to the user.
 
Return: 1 if successful, else 0.
 
 
 
 
Example
 
For an example, select the Example link above.


========================================
TOPIC: STATION_FILTER_Example.htm
========================================
STATION_FILTER example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
STATION_FILTER Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This example filters alarm viewers so that the currently logged user only accesses alarms matching the populations PopuDomain01 and PopuDomain02.
 
SUB PopuDomain01or02()
 
'Declare
 
DIM hBuffer as Long;
 
DIM strPopu as Str;
 
DIM intLength as Integer;
 
hBuffer = ALLOC_BUFFER(255);
 
strPopu = "FILTER,0,1,PopuDomain01,PopuDomain02";
 
PUT_BUFFER(hBuffer, 0, strPopu);
 
Station_filter("APPLY", hBuffer);
 
FREE_BUFFER(hBuffer);
 
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: String_Manipn.htm
========================================
Character string manipulation instructions
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
String Manipulation
Instructions 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Instruction
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
ADDSTRING
 
 
 
 
Concatenation of two character strings.
 
 
 

 
 
ASC
 
 
 
 
Return the ASCII code of the first character of a string. 
 
 
 
 

 
 
ASCIIFIELD
 
 
 
 
Retrieve, from an ASCII buffer, ASCII fields separated 
 by a given character. 
 
 
 

 
 
CHR
 
 
 
 
Return the ASCII character of the code passed as an argument. 
 
 
 
 

 
 
CMPSTRING
 
 
 
 
Compare two character strings. 
 
 
 

 
 
FORMAT
 
 
 
 
Returns a string including an inserted formatted value.
 
 
 

 
 
LCASE
 
 
 
 
Convert all the characters of a string to lower case.
 
 
 

 
 
LEFT
 
 
 
 
Return the first N characters of a string.
 
 
 

 
 
LEN
 
 
 
 
Return the length of a string.
 
 
 

 
 
LTRIM
 
 
 
 
Return a copy of a string without any leading spaces.
 
 
 

 
 
MID
 
 
 
 
Return a sub-string of a character string.
 
 
 

 REPLACE
 
 
 
Search for, and replace, a sub-string.
 
 
 

 
 
RIGHT
 
 
 
 
Return the last N characters of the string.
 
 
 

 
 
RTRIM
 
 
 
 
Return a copy of a string without any trailing spaces.
 
 
 

 
 
SPACE
 
 
 
 
Return a string composed of N spaces.
 
 
 

 
 
STRING
 
 
 
 
Return a string composed of N instances of a character.
 
 
 

 
 
TEXTVAR
 
 
 
 
Manipulate character strings.
 
 
 

 
 
UCASE
 
 
 
 
Convert all the characters of a string to upper case. 
 
 
 
 
 

 
String separator characters must be prefixed with a the backslash ("\") escape character so that they are interpreted correctly:
 
\\ for the backslash
 
\' for the simple quote
 
\" for the double quote
 
\t for the tab
 
\r for the carriage return
 
\n for the new line
 
 For example to include a simple quote in a string:
 
"a quote needs to be escaped, if not, it doesn\'t work"


========================================
TOPIC: String_record_format.htm
========================================
String record format
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
String Record Format
 

 
When you translate texts to another language, the corresponding Style Index entries must be set up also. In the simplest case that consists of copying the column 2 cells to the right of an existing language.
 
 
Lines starting with the keyword MIMIC, TEMPLATE or SYMBOL
 contain the information about one text string in each of the languages.
 Each line is of the following format.
 
Keyword,String ID,String in language 1,String in language 2,...String in languagen
 
Keyword
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Field
 
 
 
Description
 
 
 
Data type
 
 
 
Value
 
 
 

 
 
1
 
 
 
Key
 
 
 
Text
 
 
 
MIMIC, TEMPLATE or SYMBOL depending on the source of the
 string.
 
 
 
 
String ID
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Field
 
 
 
Description
 
 
 
Data type
 
 
 
Value
 
 
 

 
 
2
 
 
 
File ID
 
 
 
Text
 
 
 
The name of the file (mimic, template or symbol), including
 the library reference, to which the string element belongs. See below.
 
 
 

 
 
3
 
 
 
Text string ID
 
 
 
String
 
 
 
The unique identity of the text string within the file.
 See the topic Text
 string IDs.
 
 
 

 
 
4
 
 
 
Reserved
 
 
 
-
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
 
String for language n
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Field
 
 
 
Description
 
 
 
Data type
 
 
 
Value
 
 
 

 
 
1+(n*4)
 
 
 
The string in language n
 
 
 
Text
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
 
2+(n*4)
 
 
 
String defined
 
 
 
Numeric
 
 
 

 Always 1. 
 
 
 
 

 
 
3+(n*4)
 
 
 
Style index
 
 
 
Numeric
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
 
4+(n*4)
 
 
 
Reserved
 
 
 
-
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
 
Default settings
 

 
For text elements that have no font and format specified, the style index is set to 0.
 
 
Syntax for the File ID field
 
The File ID field contains the name of library in which
 it is located and the name of the file (mimic, template or symbol) separated
 by an oblique (/).
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Source
 
 
 
Manner of reference
 
 
 
Example
 
 
 

 Local library
 
 
File name only.
 
 
 
Sym1
 
 
 

 
 
Common library
 
 Separator before the file name.
 /Sym3
The symbol file Sym3 in the common library.
 
 

 
 
Project library
 
 Separator between library name and file name.
 ProjLib/Sym2
The symbol file Sym2 in the project library ProjLib.
 
 

 Shared library
 Separator before library name and between library name
 and file name.
 /MyLib/Sym4
The symbol file Sym4 in the shared library MyLib.
 
 
 
String record example
 
In this example, the file ID is MIMIC1 and the Text string
 ID is TEXT1.TEXT. There are strings defined for two languages. 
 
MIMIC,"MIMIC1",TEXT1.TEXT,0,"This is text in a mimic",1,1,0,"C'est un texte dans un synoptique",1,1,0


========================================
TOPIC: Style_record_format.htm
========================================
Style record format
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Style Record Format
 
The style (font and format properties) of a text string 
 is not recorded as part of the text string record. Instead, it is 
 indexed and recorded separately. A text string record refers to the style 
 via the style index, so that when several text strings are defined with 
 the same style, the style is only recorded once. 
 
The fields of a font and format record are as follows:
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 Field
 Description
 Data type
 Value
 
 

 1
 Record ID
 Text
 FONT
 
 

 2
 Style index
 Numeric
 1 to n
 
 

 3
 Height - see table below
 Numeric
 -11 to -96
 
 

 4
 Width
 Numeric
 &#160;
 
 

 5
 Weight
 Numeric
 Bold = 700 Normal = 400
 
 

 6
 Italic
 Boolean
 0 or 1
 
 

 7
 Underlined
 Boolean
 0 or 1
 
 

 8
 Font name
 Text
 "Arial" etc.
 
 

 9
 Strikeout
 Boolean
 0 or 1
 
 

 10
 Character set
 Numeric
 Western = 0, Cyrillic = 204 
 
 

 11
 Format - text alignment - see table below
 Numeric
 36 to 86
 
 
 
 
The Height field
 
The actual font height can be ascertained using the following look-up table. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 Height code
 Font size
 &#160;
 Height code
 Font size
 
 

 -11
 8
 &#160;
 -27
 20
 
 

 -12
 9
 &#160;
 -29
 22
 
 

 -13
 10
 &#160;
 -32
 24
 
 

 -15
 11
 &#160;
 -35
 26
 
 

 -16
 12
 &#160;
 -37
 28
 
 

 -19
 14
 &#160;
 -48
 36
 
 

 -21
 16
 &#160;
 -64
 48
 
 

 -24
 18
 &#160;
 -96
 72
 
 
 
 
The Format field
 
 
 
 
 

 Format code
 Text alignment
 
 

 36
 Left
 
 

 37
 Centered
 
 

 38
 Right
 
 

 84
 Multi-line and left
 
 

 85
 Multi-line and centered
 
 

 86
 Multi-line and right
 
 
 
 
Example
 
This is an example of a Font and Format record: 
 
FONT,1,-21,0,400,0,0,"Arial",0,1,37


========================================
TOPIC: SUB_ENDSUB.htm
========================================
SUB_ENDSUB
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SUB...ENDSUB
 

 
 

 

 

 See also 

Example
 
Define the start and end of a function.
 
WebVue support - Yes.
 
Syntax
 
SUB subname([P1, 
 P2 ......P10])
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;[instruction list]
 
ENDSUB
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
P1 to P10
 
 
 
Parameters to be passed to the function. All 
 types supported.
 
 
 
 
Execution
 
A function subname 
 is created. The parameters are not type cast but they must match the use 
 within the function or a run time error will occur.
 

 
It is obligatory to have one MAIN routine per program 
 (except for the global declaration program). 
 
 
A value may be returned from a function using RETURN.
 
Example
 
For an example, select the Example link above.


========================================
TOPIC: SUBENDSUB_Example.htm
========================================
SUB...ENDSUB example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SUB END SUB Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This example shows a main SUB calling procedures. There are also calls among these and other procedures, with and without parameters.
 
SUB Main()
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
&#160;
 
procedure1();
 
procedure2("parameter1");
 
intResult = procedure4();
 
PRINT("Return from Procedure4 = ",intResult);
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB procedure1()
 
'procedure without parameters
 
PRINT("Procedure1");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB procedure2(strParam1)
 
'procedure with one parameter
 
PRINT("Procedure2 parameter: ",strParam1);
 
procedure3("parameter1","parameter2");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB procedure3(strParam1,strParam2)
 
'procedure with two parameters
 
PRINT("Procedure3 parameters: ",strParam1,strParam2);
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB procedure4()
 
'procedure without parameters
 
PRINT("Procedure4");
 
Return(3);
 
END SUB
 
The results are:
 
Procedure1
 
Procedure2 parameter: parameter1
 
Procedure3 parameters : parameter1 parameter2
 
Procedure4
 
Return from Procedure4 = 3

Supported Animations
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Supported Animations
 
3D mimics support the following animations.
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Class
 
 
 
Animation
 
 
 
Comment
 
 
 

 
 
Color
 
 
 
Bit
Bit Group
Register Bit
Alarm
Register Value
Gradient Register
 
 
 
The color animations are modified to allow the selection 
 of the material and the properties of the material that are to be animated.
 
 
 

 
 
Text
 
 
 
None
 
 
 
3D mimics are unable to display text strings.
 
 
 

 
 
Symbol
 
 
 
All
 
 
 
Only for use with symbols containing 3D models.
 
 
 

 
 
Position
 
 
 
Single Axis
 
2 Axes
Rotation
 
 
 
Single/2 Axes animations for 3D provide similar functionality 
 to those for 2D.
3D Rotation provides similar functionality to the 2D Rotation 
 animation.
 
 
 

 
 
Visibility
 
 
 
All
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
 
Send
 
 
 
All
 
 &#160;
 
 

 
 
Run
 
 
 
All
 
 
 
No highlighting around the drawing element (3D model) when 
 the cursor is over it.
 
 
 

 
 
Link
 
 
 
All
 
 &#160;
 
 

 
 
Security
 
 
 
All
 
 &#160;


========================================
TOPIC: SVALA_Example.htm
========================================
SVALA Alarm Information example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SVALA (Alarm Information) Examples
 
 

Applies to
 
Example 1
 
This example exercises the SVALA command. 
 
Variables required:
 
 
@ALAEND is a variable of type Bit.
 
@NB_ALARMS is a variable of type Register.
 
 
The program also requires these global
 variables to be declared:
 
 
DIM hdbuffer As Long;
 
DIM hparam As Long;
 
 
The module "Main" must be run first to trigger
 an event before module SvAla1 is run.
 
SUB Main()
 
EVENT("ADDPROG","@ALAEND","ALL>1","sval.SCB","","putfile");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB SvAla1()
 
DIM GFilter As STR;
 
DIM GFormat As STR;
 
DIM GState1 As STR;
 
DIM GState2 As STR;
 
DIM GState3 As STR;
 
DIM GState4 As STR;
 
DIM GState5 As STR;
 
DIM GState6 As STR;
 
DIM Gminmax As STR;
 
DIM Gmin As STR;
 
DIM Gmax As STR;
 
DIM chParam As STR;
 
chParam = "";
 
hdbuffer = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 48000 );
 
hparam = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 4000 );
 
&#160;
 
' &#160;&#160;LIMITATION ON LINES
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam,"0" ); 
 
'No. of lines ( 0 - no limitations,
 
' if this is not the size of the buffer )
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' MODE taking into account MIN & MAX
 
GMinMax="0";
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GMinMax);
 
' equivalent - check all Min and Max boxes
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' MIN LEVEL 
 
Gmin = "0";
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , Gmin); 
 
'Priority� min
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' MAX LEVEL
 
GMax="29";
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GMax);
 
'Priority� max
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;TYPES OF ALARM 
 
GState1 = "1"; 'taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState1);
 
'taken into account present not ack
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
GState2 = "1"; 'taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState2);
 
'present ack
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
GState3="1"; 'taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState3);
 
'Hidden not ack
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
GState4="1"; 'taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState4);
 
'Inactive
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
GState5 = "1"; 'taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState5);
 
'Invalid
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
GState6 = "0"; 'not taken into account
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState6);
 
'User masked
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;FILTERING 
 
'GFilter = "";
 
GFilter = "=(#A1 == DOM1)||(#A1 == DOM2)";
 
'GFilter = "=(#A1 == DOM1)";
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam,GFilter);
 
'filter variables with Domain DOM1 or DOM2
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
 
'field separator
 
&#160;
 
' &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;FORMAT 
 
GFormat="#D/#M/#Y #h:#m:#s #t #@A1 #@A2";
 
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam, GFormat );
 
'Format
 
&#160;
 
' &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MODIFYING THE BUFFER OF PARAMETERS 
 
PUT_BUFFER(hparam,0,chParam);
 
PRINT("Extraced by SVALA :",SVALA(1,hdbuffer,hparam,"ALAEND",1,"NB_ALARMS"));
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB putfile()
 
BUFTOFILE (hdbuffer, "SvAlaResult","USEFULL_PART","Append");
 
FREE_BUFFER(hdbuffer);
 
FREE_BUFFER(hparam);
 
@ALAEND = 0;
 
END SUB
 
Example 2
 
This is another program to extract information on alarms.
 It uses the CYCLIC and EVENT commands to activate SVALA functions.
 
DIM hdl As LONG;
DIM hparam As LONG;
DIM hdl2 As LONG;
DIM hdl3 As LONG;
DIM GFilter As STR;
DIM GFormat As STR;
DIM Gstate1 As STR;
DIM Gstate2 As STR;
DIM Gstate3 As STR;
DIM Gstate4 As STR;
DIM Gstate5 As STR;
DIM Gstate6 As STR;
DIM Gminmax As STR;
DIM Gmin As STR;
DIM Gmax As STR;
DIM Lock As INTEGER;
 
&#160;
 
SUB Main()
hdl = 0;
hdl2 = 0;
hdl3 = 0;
hparam = 0;
Lock = 0 ;
END SUB

 
SUB AlarmExtract()
If ( Lock == 1 ) Then
PRINT ( "request in progress " );
CYCLIC ( "DEL" , 2 , "SvAla" ,"", "Filter2" );
CYCLIC ( "ADDPROG" , 2 , "SvAla" ,"", "Filter2" );
Return;
End If
Lock = 1 ;
@STATE = 0;
@ANA = 0;
GFilter = "";
GFilter = ADDSTRING(GFilter, @FILTER);
GFormat = "";
GFormat = ADDSTRING(GFormat, @ALAFORMAT);
GMinMax = FORMAT ( "%d" , @MINMAX );
GMin1 = FORMAT ( "%d" , @MIN );
GMin2 = FORMAT ( "%d" , @MAX );
GState1 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT1 );
GState2 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT2 );
GState3 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT3 );
GState4 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT4 );
GState5 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT5 );
GState6 =FORMAT ( "%d" , @ETAT6 );
DoAlarmExtract();
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB DoAlarmExtract()
DIM i As Integer;
DIM chParam As Str;
chParam = "";
If ( hdl == 0 ) Then
hdl = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 16000 );
End If
If ( hparam == 0 ) Then
hparam = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 2000 );
End If
 
&#160; 
 
' Number of lines. 0 = no limitation
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam,"0," ); '
' Level filter (0,1 or 2)
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GMinMax
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Minimum priority
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , Gmin)
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Maximum priority
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GMax);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Alarms on and not acknowledged
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState1);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Alarms on and acknowledged
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState2);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Alarms off and not acknowledged
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState3);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Alarms off
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState4);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Invalid alarms
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState5);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Alarms masked by the operator
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , GState6);
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam , ",");
' Filter - Alarms of nature ELEC and starting with the name WORKSHOP
 
GFilter = "=(#t BEG WORKSHOP)&&(#A2 == ELEC)";
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam,GFilter);
' Line format
chParam = ADDSTRING(chParam, GFormat );
PUT_BUFFER(hparam,0,chParam);
i=EVENT("ADDPROG","ETAT",1,"SVALA","","RcvEvt" );
i = SVALA ( "EXTRACT" , hdl, hparam , "ETAT" , 1 , "ANA" );
If ( i < 1 ) Then
PRINT (" i " , i );
End If
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
SUB RcvEvt()
DIM i As Integer;
DIM j As Integer;
DIM p As Str;
DIM sep As Str;
DIM sep2 As Str;
DIM lig As Integer;
sep = "\n";
sep2 = ",";
If ( hdl2 == 0 ) Then
hdl2 = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 1000 );
End If
If ( hdl3 == 0 ) Then
hdl3 = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 1000 );
End If
SEQ_BUFFER ( "BEGIN" , hdl ); ' Start of buffer
i = 1;
While ( i != 0)
i = SEQ_BUFFER ( "NEXTFIELD" , hdl , sep , hdl2, 1000);
p = CGET_BUFFER ( hdl2 , 0 , 255 );
j = SEQ_BUFFER ( "BEGIN" , hdl2 );
j = 1;
While ( j != 0)
j = SEQ_BUFFER ( "NEXTFIELD" , hdl2 , sep2 , hdl3 , 1000 );
p = CGET_BUFFER ( hdl3 , 0 , 255 );
Wend
Wend
Lock = 0 ;
i=EVENT("DEL","STATE",1);
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: SVBRANCH_Example.htm
========================================
SVBRANCH example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SVBRANCH Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This program illustrates the use of the SVBRANCH instruction.
 
Example prerequisites
 
 

 
One or more variables in a branch called @SB_EXAMPLES are required. If changing the variable values manually the Command attribute must be set.
 
 

 
To set the variables and visualize the results of using the program, a mimic displaying the variable values and status is useful (using the Grid control in variable tracking mode for example).
 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 
'-------- Program wide variables
 
 
 

 
DIM sBranch As STR;
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
SUB Main ()
 
 
 

 
'-------- Initialize variables
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;sBranch = "SB_EXAMPLES"; 'The name of the branch to use
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Disable all variables in the branch sBranch
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_DISABLE()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("DISABLE", sBranch);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Disable branch ", sBranch, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Disable branch ", sBranch, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Enable all variables in the branch sBranch
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_ENABLE()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("ENABLE", sBranch);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Enable branch ", sBranch, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Enable branch ", sBranch, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Mask all variables in the branch sBranch, using a mask level passed as an argument
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_MASK ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER,iLevel As INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Get mask passsed as an argument&#160;&#160;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iLevel = IVAL(GETARG("ARG1"));
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("MASK", sBranch, iLevel);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Mask level ", iLevel, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Mask level ", iLevel, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Unmask all variables in the branch sBranch, using a mask level passed as an argument
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_UNMASK ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER,iLevel As INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Get mask passsed as an argument&#160;&#160;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iLevel = IVAL(GETARG("ARG1"));
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("UNMASK", sBranch, iLevel);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("UnMask level ", iLevel, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("UnMask level ", iLevel, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Temporarily change the alarm level of all alarms in the branch sBranch, using an alarm level passed as an argument
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_SETALARMLEVEL ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER,iLevel As INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Get new alarm level passsed as an argument&#160;&#160;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iLevel = IVAL(GETARG("ARG1"));
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("SETALARMLEVEL", sBranch, iLevel);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Set alarm level ", iLevel, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Set alarm level ", iLevel, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Restore temporary alarm levels of all alarms in the branch sBranch (level set with SETALARMLEVEL)
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_RESTOREALARMLEVEL ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("RESTOREALARMLEVEL", sBranch);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Restore alarm level successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Restore alarm level failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Toggle all alarms in the branch sBranch, from ON to OFF to ON&#160;
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_RESETANDSETALARMBRANCH ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("RESETANDSETALARM", sBranch);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Reset and set alarm branch ",sBranch, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Reset and set alarm branch ",sBranch, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB
 
 
 

 
&#160;
 
 
 

 
'-------- Toggle all alarms that pass the sFilter filter, from ON to OFF to ON&#160;
 
 
 

 
SUB Exec_RESETANDSETALARMFILTER ()
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM iReturn AS INTEGER;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;DIM sFilter As STR;
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;sFilter = "=(#A1 == DOM1)";
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;iReturn = SVBRANCH("RESETANDSETALARM", "", sFilter);
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;IF(iReturn == 1) THEN
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Reset and set alarm with filter ",sFilter, " successful");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PRINT("Reset and set alarm with filter ",sFilter, " failed");
 
 
 

 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
 
 

 
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: SVLOG_Example.htm
========================================
SVLOG example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SVLOG Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This example shows the use of the SVLOG instruction to extract data logged in the previous hour from the Log Filter LIST1.
 
' Constants for log data type:

const VALTRANSEV = 1; 'Bit transition
const ALARMACQEV = 2; 'Alarm acknowledge
const WRITECMDEEV = 4; 'Send a command
const WRITECTRLEV = 8; 'Send a control value
const WRITETEXTEV = 16; 'Send text
const WRITERECIPESEV = 32; 'Send a recipe
const STARTEXP = 128; 'Log on
const STOPEXP = 256; 'Log off
const TRANSETAT_0 = 64; 'Bit transition to 0
const TRANSETAT_1 = 512; 'Bit transition to 1
const TRANSETAT_NS = 1024; ' Bit transition to NS
const TRANSETAT_ALLALL = 1600; 'All bit transitions
const TRANSALA_PRSACQ = 2048; 'Alarm On Ack
const TRANSALA_PRSNOACQ = 4096; 'Alarm On Nack
const TRANSALA_ABSACQ = 8192; 'Alarm Off
const TRANSALA_ABSNOACQ = 16384; 'Alarm Off Nack
const TRANSALA_NS = 32768; 'Alarm NS.
const TRANSALA_ACQOPERATOR = 65536; 'Operator ack
const TRANSALA_ALLALL = 63488; 'All alm transitions
const FORCAGEPROG = 131072; 'Run program
const TRANSALA_OFF = 262144; 'Alarm off
const TRANSALA_ON = 524288; 'Alarm on
 
&#160;
 
' Global variable
&#160; DIM HandleDest As long; 'Buffer handle

SUB Main()
&#160; DIM Ret as long;
&#160; DIM HandleParam as long;
&#160; DIM Hd1 as double;
&#160; DIM Hd2 as double;
&#160; DIM Mask as long;
&#160; DIM ChParam as STR;
&#160; EVENT ("ADDPROG", "Extract.Fin", 1, "LOG", "", "EndExtract");
&#160; ' Create the filter parameters buffer

&#160; HandleParam = alloc_buffer(1000);
&#160; Mask=0;
&#160; Mask = LOGICAL("OR",Mask, WRITECTRLEV);
&#160; Mask = LOGICAL("OR",Mask, TRANSETAT_ALLALL);
&#160; Hd1 = DateTimeValue();
&#160; Hd2 = Hd1 - 3600000;
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,"LIST1");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, format("%.0f",Hd2));
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, format("%.0f",Hd1));
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "0"); ' Max lines
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "0"); ' Direction
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, TOC(Mask)); ' Mask
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "0"); ' Min priority
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "15"); ' Max priority
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; ' Selection filter
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "=(#A1 == DOM1)||(#A1 == DOM2)");
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam,",");
&#160; ' Output format
&#160; chParam = addstring(chParam, "#h:#m:#s #E #O #T #t #C #c #d #n");
&#160; put_buffer(HandleParam,0,chParam);
&#160; ' Create the output buffer
 ' ----------------------------------------------------
&#160; HandleDest = alloc_buffer(20000);
&#160; ' Execute the extract function 
' ----------------------------------------------------
&#160; Ret = SvLog("Extract", HandleDest, HandleParam, "Extract.Fin","Extract.Status",
 
 "Extract.Lignes", "Extract.HDeb","Extract.HFin");
&#160; If (Ret == 0) then
&#160; PRINT ("Error in extract function");
&#160; Event ("DelProg", "EndExtract", 1);
&#160; End if
&#160; ' Free buffers
&#160; Free_Buffer(HandleParam);
END SUB

' Function to be executed when extraction is complete

SUB EndExtract()
&#160; DIM NomApp as STR;
&#160; DIM Ret as INTEGER;
&#160; PRINT ("Extract complete");
&#160; If (Extract.Status == 0) then
&#160; PRINT ("All lines extracted");
 Else
 &#160;&#160;PRINT ("Buffer full - some data may be missing");
 End if
 PRINT ("The first archive date is ", DateTimeString(Extract.HDeb));
 PRINT ("The last archive date is ", DateTimeString (Extract.HFin));
 
&#160;
 
' Convert the buffer to a file
 
&#160;
 
SEQ_BUFFER("CRTOCRLF", HandleDest);
 
BUFTOFILE(HandleDest,"Extract.txt", "USEFULL_PART");
 
FREE_BUFFER(HandleDest);
 
&#160;
 
END SUB

AIGrid object 
 
 
Grid Object
&#160;

Example&#160;

Properties&#160;

Methods&#160;

Events&#160;
The AIGrid object
 represents the Grid control.
Changing the Grid by program is only a temporary operation. When the
 mimic containing it is closed, the changes are lost.

TOPIC: svobjAutoResizeX.htm
========================================
Auto Resize Grid control example 
 
 
Auto Resize Grid control Example
This script automatically adapts the size of the Grid control so that 
 all cells and rows are visible.
Private Sub Shape2_Click()
AIGrid1.Width = AIGrid1.TotalWidth
AIGrid1.Height = AIGrid1.TotalHeight
End Sub
&#160;


========================================
TOPIC: svobjAutoSizeP.htm
========================================
AutoSize property 
 
 
AutoSize Property
&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Description
Resizes the cells to fit the surround. Write-only.
Syntax
object.AutoSize 
 As Boolean
Example
AIGrid1.AutoSize = True
All rows will have the same height, equal 
 to the available height divided by the number of rows.
All columns will have the same width, equal to the available width divided 
 by the number of columns.
Any previous settings of cell sizes will be overridden.

TOPIC: svobjBackColorP.htm

BackColor property 
 
 
BackColor Property
&#160;

Example&#160;&#160;&#160; &#160;

Applies to&#160;
Description
Returns or sets the background color of the viewer's list. Read/Write.
Syntax
object.BackColor As OLEColor
The color value is returned as a number e.g. 65535. It can be set in
 the same way or by passing a constant:
In the Object Browser, the class ColorConstants
 in the VBA library contains constant values for setting BackColor,
 e.g. 'vbYellow'. 
Example
Trend1.BackColor = RGB(128, 128, 128)

TOPIC: svobjBasicChangeFilterM.htm
========================================
BasicChangeFilter method
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 BasicChangeFilter Method
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to&#160;
 
Adds or removes a filter condition.
 
Syntax
 
object.BasicChangeFilter 
 (aiFilter As 
 Integer, abValue As Boolean) As 
 Boolean
 
 

 
 
Part
 
 
 
 
Description
 
 
 
 

 
 
aiFilter
 
 
 
 
Which kind of event to add or remove:
 
0 = &#160;ALARM_ACK_OFF
 
1 = &#160;ALARM_ACK_ON 
 
 
2 = &#160;ALARM_NACK_ON
 
3 = &#160;ALARM_NACK_OFF
 
4 = &#160;ALARM_MASKED 
 
 
5 = &#160;ALARM_NS 
 
 
6 = &#160;ALARM_PROGMASKED 
 
 
7 = &#160;ALARM_VARMASKED 
 
 
8 = &#160;ALARM_INHIB 
 
 
9 = &#160;ALARM_NA 
 
 
10 = ALARM_EXPRMASKED
 
11 = ALARM_MAINTENANCEMASKED 
 
 
 

 
 
abValue
 
 
 
 
Whether to add or remove this event type.
 
 
 
 
It is not possible to combine event types 
 in one command (e.g. ALARM_ACK_OFF with ALARM_NACK_ON). Instead, execute 
 this method once for each type.

========================================
TOPIC: svobjFirstSelectedCellM.htm
========================================
FirstSelectedCell method 
 
 
FirstSelectedCell Method
&#160;

See also&#160;

Example&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Sets the internal cell pointer to the first selected
 cell if any. 
Syntax
Function FirstSelectedCell() As Boolean
The internal cell pointer is not necessarily the same as
 that pointed to by the CurrentCellColumn and CurrentCellRow properties.
Returns True if at least one cell is selected, otherwise False.
Calling this function makes an internal copy of the current selection for
 later enumeration.
If the selection changes, you should call this method again.
See also the SelectedCellRow and SelectedCellCol properties to retrieve
 the cell indicated by the internal pointer.
See also the NextSelectedCell method to get the next cell in the selection.

TOPIC: svobjGetCursorValueM.htm
========================================
GetCursorValue method 
 
 
GetCursorValue Method
&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Get the Value and Quality for a curve, at the cursor point. 
Syntax
Function GetCursorValue (nID As Integer, value As Double, valid As Boolean)&#160;

Part
Description

nID
Curve number.

value
Value at the cursor.

valid
True if value at the cursor is valid, else False.
Returns False if the cursor is not currently displayed.
Example
 Dim curveID As Integer
 
Dim value As Double 
Dim valid As Boolean

 
bok = Trend1.GetCursorValue(curveID, value, valid)

========================================
TOPIC: svobjLoopAllSelectedCellsX.htm
========================================
Loop Through Grid control example 
 
 
Loop Through Grid control Example
If any cells are selected, this script loops through them so as to display 
 each cell in turn.
Private Sub Shape1_Click()
If AIGrid1.FirstSelectedCell 
 Then
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MsgBox 
 AIGrid1.SelectedCellRow & AIGrid1.SelectedCellCol
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;While 
 AIGrid1.NextSelectedCell
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MsgBox 
 AIGrid1.SelectedCellRow & AIGrid1.SelectedCellCol
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Wend
Else
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MsgBox 
 "No selection"
End If
End Sub
&#160;

========================================
TOPIC: svobjNextSelectedCellM.htm
========================================
NextSelectedCell method 
 
 
NextSelectedCell Method
&#160;

See also&#160;

Example&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Sets the internal cell pointer to the next selected 
 cell if any.
Syntax
Function NextSelectedCell() As Boolean
Returns True if there is another cell in the selection, otherwise False.
Calling this function makes an internal copy of the current selection for 
 later enumeration.
If the selection changes, you should call this method again.
See also the SelectedCellRow and SelectedCellCol properties to retrieve 
 the cell indicated by the internal pointer.

TOPIC: svobjScrollBarsP.htm
========================================
ScrollBars property 
 
 
Scrollbars Property
&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Description
Sets or gets the visibility mode of the scrollbars. Read/Write.
Syntax
object.Scrollbars
 As ScrollBarMode
where ScrollBarMode takes these
 values:

Value
Effect

sbNone
Never display the scrollbars.

sbHorizontal
Always display the horizontal scrollbar. 

sbVertical
Always display the vertical scrollbar.

sbBoth
Always display both scrollbars.

sbAutomatic
Display scrollbars automatically when all cells cannot be displayed
 at once.


========================================
TOPIC: svobjScrollM.htm
========================================
Scroll method 
 
 
Scroll Method
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;

Applies to &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; 
Scrolls the object vertically.
Syntax
Sub Scroll(ScrollMode 
 As ScrollMode, Line 
 As Long)
The value of ScrollMode controls 
 the manner of scrolling.
smToTop: go to top (equivalent to Home key)
smPageUp: up one page (equivalent to PageUp key)
smLineUp: up one line
smLineDown: down one line
smPageDown: down one page (equivalent to PageDown key)
smToBottom: go to bottom (equivalent to End key)
smToLine: go to line number Line: 
 it will be displayed as the first line
The Line parameter is used 
 for the smToLine mode only.

Get/set the threshold system for a curve.
As a SET:
-1 - no Threshold

0 - Automatic (automatic according to variable configuration, only in SET mode)

1 - ppphi, pphi, phi, hi

2 - pphi, phi, hi, lo

3 - phi, hi, lo, plo

4 - hi, lo, plo, pplo 
5 - lo, plo, pplo, ppplo

As a GET:

-1 - no Threshold

1 - ppphi, pphi, phi, hi

2 - pphi, phi, hi, lo

3 - phi, hi, lo, plo

4 - hi, lo, plo, pplo 
5 - lo, plo, pplo, ppplo
Syntax
object.ThresholdMode as Integer&#160;
Example
aCurve.ThresholdMode = 0

Trend Viewer example 
 
 
Trend Viewer Example
This example exercises a method and several properties of the Trend
 Viewer:
Private Sub Run_VBA_for_Trend_Click()
&#160;
'METHOD:
&#160;
'show the About box:
Trend1.AboutBox
&#160;
'PROPERTIES:
&#160;
'change background colour
 to yellow then blue
Trend1.BackColor = vbYellow
MsgBox ("Background
 colour was: " & Trend1.BackColor)
Trend1.BackColor = vbBlue
&#160;
'toggle the time information
 below the scale bar:
Trend1.DisplayTimeInfo
 = Not Trend1.DisplayTimeInfo
&#160;
'toggle the play mode
 (play/pause):
Trend1.PlayMode = Not
 Trend1.PlayMode
&#160;
'change the refresh period:
MsgBox ("Refresh
 Period was: " & Trend1.RefreshPeriod)
Trend1.RefreshPeriod =
 Trend1.RefreshPeriod * 2
MsgBox ("Refresh
 Period is now: " & Trend1.RefreshPeriod)
&#160;
'toggle the scale bar
 (visible/hidden):
Trend1.scalebar = Not
 Trend1.scalebar
&#160;
'toggle the scale line
 (visible/hidden):
Trend1.ScaleLine = Not
 Trend1.ScaleLine
&#160;
'toggle the toolbar (visible/hidden):
Trend1.toolbar = Not Trend1.toolbar
&#160;
MsgBox ("To show
 or hide the bars, run the script again.")
&#160;
End Sub

TOPIC: svobjVariableFilterM.htm
========================================
VariableFilter method 
 
 
VariableFilter Method
&#160;

Applies to&#160;
Changes the object to show each variable's name, title 
 or both.
Syntax
Sub VariableFilter(Filter 
 As String, Type 
 As VariableType)
Filter corresponds to the regular 
 expression in the Variables tab of the object's Drawing Properties dialog 
 .
Type specifies the type 
 of variable:
vtAlarm - Alarm
vtBit - Bit
vtRegister - Register
vtText - Text
These can be combined with the '+' operator, 
 for example 'vtAlarm+vtRegister'.
This mode only works in 
 variable tracking mode (with the Variable Tracking property selected).
This method is equivalent to 
 the SCADA Basic verb SELECTOR, mode VARIABLE.

 As VariableMode)

Filter corresponds to the regular 
 expression in the Variables tab of the object's Drawing Properties dialog.

 of variable:

vtBit - Bit
vtRegister - Register
vtText - Text

 for example 'vtAlarm+vtRegister'.

========================================
TOPIC: SVSQL_Example.htm
========================================
SVSQL Database example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SVSQL Database Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This example uses a mimic (SQLTEST) containing two arrays 
 (TITLE and DATA) in which the results of executing a SQL command are placed. 
 
 
The User enters the name of the database in the text 
 variable @DATABASE.NAME and the SQL command in the variable @DATABASE.SQL. 
 The variable @DATABASE.ERROR is used to report the status and any error 
 to the User.
 
DIM HBase as long; 'Handle for database connection
DIM RetSQL as long; 'Return from SVSQL command

SUB Main ()
&#160; HBase = 0;
END SUB

SUB Connect()
@DATABASE.ERROR = ADDSTRING("Connecting to database", UCase(@DATABASE.NAME));
Delay(0.1);
ClearArray();
If (HBase > 0) Then
&#160; &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Database already connected";
Return(0);
Else
 If (CmpString(@DATABASE.NAME, "") ==0) Then
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Please enter database name";
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 Else
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;HBase = SVSQL("CONNECT",@DATABASE.NAME);
 End If
End If
If (HBase > 0) then
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Database connected OK";
Else
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Database failed to connect";
End If
Print(Hbase);
END SUB

&#160;

SUB Disconnect()
 Print (Hbase);
 @DATABASE.ERROR = AddString("Disconnecting database " , UCase(@DATABASE.NAME));
 Delay(0.1);
 If (HBase <= 0) Then
 @DATABASE.ERROR = "Database not connected";
 &#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 End if
 RetSQL = SVSQL("DISCONNECT",HBase);
 If (RetSQL == -1) Then
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Error disconnecting database";
 &#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 Else
 &#160;&#160;If (RetSQL == -2) Then
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Incorrect database handle";
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 &#160;&#160;Else
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Disconnected OK";
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;Hbase = 0;
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;ClearArray();
 &#160;&#160;End If
 End If
END SUB

SUB ExecuteSQL()
 DIM HBufErr as long; ' Handle of error buffer
 DIM HField as long; ' Handle of SQL buffer
 DIM NbTableCol As Integer;
 DIM NbCol As Long;
 DIM i As Integer;
 DIM j As Integer;
 Const NbMaxRow = 15;
 Const NbMaxCol = 5;

&#160;

&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Executing query";
 Delay(0.1);
 If (HBase <= 0) Then
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = "Database not connected";
 &#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 End If

' Initialise buffers

 HBufErr = alloc_buffer(255);
 HField = alloc_buffer(255);
 ClearArray();

' Search for information

 RetSQL = SVSQL("EXECUTE", HBase, @DATABASE.SQL);
 If (RetSQL < 0) Then
 &#160;&#160;RetSQL = SVSQL("ERROR", HBase , HBufErr);
 &#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = Cget_Buffer(HBufErr, 0, RetSQL, 1);
 &#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 Else
 &#160;&#160;NbCol = SVSQL("NUMCOL", HBase);
 End if
 If (NbCol > NbMaxCol) then
 &#160;&#160;NbTableCol = NbMaxCol;
 Else
 &#160;&#160;NbTableCol = TOI(NbCol);
 End if

' Get column names from table

&#160;For (i=1 ; i<=NbTableCol; i++)
 &#160;&#160;RetSQL = SVSQL("GETCOLNAME", HBase , I, HField);
 &#160;&#160;If (RetSQL < 0) then
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RetSQL = SVSQL("ERROR", HBase , HBufErr);
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;@DATABASE.ERROR = Cget_Buffer(HBufErr, 0, RetSQL, 1);
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RETURN(0);
 &#160;&#160;End If
 &#160;&#160;SELECTOR("PUTCELL", "SQLTEST", "", "TITLE", 0, i-1, Cget_Buffer(HField, 0, RetSQL, 1));
 Next i;

&#160;For (i=NbTableCol+1 ; i<=5; i++)
 SELECTOR("PUTCELL", "SQLTEST", "", "TITLE", 0, i-1, "---");
 Next i;

' Retrieve values and write to table

 For(i=1; i<=NbMaxRow; i++)
 &#160;&#160;RetSQL = SVSQL("FETCH", HBase);
 &#160;&#160;If (RetSQL != 0) Then
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;BREAK();
 &#160;&#160;End If
 &#160;&#160;DELAY(0.1);
 &#160;&#160;For (j=1 ; j<=NbTableCol; j++)
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;RetSQL = SVSQL("GETCOL", HBase , j, HField);
 &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;SELECTOR("PUTCELL", "SQLTEST", "", "DATA", i-1, j-1, Cget_Buffer(HField, 0, RetSQL, 1));
 &#160;&#160;Next j;
 Next i;
 @DATABASE.ERROR = "Query complete";
END SUB

SUB ClearArray()
 SELECTOR("CLEAR", "SQLTEST", "", "TITLE");
 SELECTOR("CLEAR", "SQLTEST", "", "DATA");
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: SVTREND_Example.htm
========================================
SVTREND example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SVTREND Examples
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
Example 1
 
This program extracts trend (Historic) data for a list 
 of variables, between two dates.
 
SUB GetTrend()
DIM d_Date1 As Double;
DIM d_Date2 As Double;
DIM d_Period As Double;
DIM l_Res As Long;
DIM s_Misc As Str;
gn_Ind = 0;
d_Date1 = DATETIMEVALUE("16/02/2003", "08:00:00:000");
d_Date2 = DATETIMEVALUE("17/02/2003", "21:00:00:000");
If ( l_Result == 0 ) Then
 l_Result = ALLOC_BUFFER ( 8000 );
End If
 d_Period = 3600;
 s_Div = " ,,@STATE,@CURVAR";
 l_Res = SVTREND ( "GETTREND" , "STATE1,STATE2,MES1,MES2,MES3,MES4,MES5,MES6", 
 
&#160;&#160;"TREND",d_Date1, d_Date2, d_Period, 2, l_Result, s_Misc );
END SUB

 
SUB GetNext()
DIM l_Res As Long;
DIM s As Str;
gn_Ind++;
PRINT ("Pass", gn_Ind );
PRINT ("CURVAR" , @CURVAR );
s = CGET_BUFFER(l_Result , 0 , 1 );
If (CMPSTRING (s, "") != 0) Then
PRINT ("WRITE");
BUFTOFILE(l_Result, TOC(gn_Ind), "USEFULL_PART" );
@STATE = 0;
l_Res = SVTREND ( "GETNEXTBUFFER" , l_Result );
End If
END SUB
 

 
When the buffer is full, the state variable LogVar 
 is set to 1. 
 
 
That allows an event to be triggered (see the verb EVENT) so as to restart the extraction 
 of the remaining data. When there are no more data to extract, Handle_Result 
 becomes null.
 
When DisplayMode 
 is 1, the data are extracted using the list of variables in the buffer 
 Handle_Listvar to the format 
 defined by Format.
 
Data example 1
 
This shows the contents of the buffer, using a sampling 
 period of 1 second in Mode 1. There 
 were four variable names in the buffer pointed to by ListHandle.
 
StrVar = STATE1:
 
20030226145546675,0 
20030226145547675,0 
20030226145548675,0 
20030226145549675,1 
20030226145550675,0 
20030226145551675,1
 
StrVar = STATE2:
 
20030226145546675, 1 
20030226145547675, 1 
20030226145548675, 0 
20030226145549675, 1 
20030226145550675, 0 
20030226145551675, 0
 
StrVar = ANA1: 
 
 
20030226145546675, -0.927184 
20030226145547675, -0.927184 
20030226145548675, -0.898794 
20030226145549675, -0.882948 
20030226145550675, -0.882948 
20030226145551675, -0.882948
 
StrVar = ANA2: 
 
 
20030226145546675, 64.99774 
20030226145547675, 65.007904 
20030226145548675, 65.018062 
20030226145549675, 65.028214 
20030226145550675, 65.038361 
20030226145551675, 65.038361
 
When DisplayMode 
 is 2, the sampled data for each variable will be contained on the same 
 line preceded by its time and date stamp.
 
Data example 2
 
The sampling period was 1000mS (1 second). There were 
 four variable names in the buffer pointed to by ListHandle.
 
20030226145546675,0,1,-0.927184,64.99774 
20030226145547675,0,1,-0.927184,65.007904 
20030226145548675,0,0,-0.898794,65.018062 
20030226145549675,1,1,-0.882948,65.028214 
20030226145550675,0,0,-0.882948,65.038361 
20030226145551675,1,0,-0.882948, 65.038361
 
Example 2
 
This example calculates mean, minimum and maximum temperatures.
 
REGISTERS.DAT is a file to hold the names of register 
 variables to be processed.
 
const MAX_VARIABLES = 1;
 
const BUFSIZE = 10000; ' size of buffer to take the data
 
DIM hDest AS LONG;
 
DIM hNames AS LONG;
 
DIM hBranch AS LONG;
 
DIM hVariables AS LONG;
 
DIM nb_variables AS INTEGER;
 
DIM nbPoints [8] AS SINGLE;
 
DIM SumValue [8] AS SINGLE;
 
DIM MinValue [8] AS SINGLE;
 
DIM MaxValue [8] AS SINGLE;
 
DIM AveValue [8] AS SINGLE;
 
DIM flagEnd AS INTEGER;
 
DIM filesource AS STR;
 
DIM lock AS INTEGER;
 
&#160;
 
SUB main ()
 
hDest = alloc_buffer (BUFSIZE);
 
filesource = "REGISTERS.DAT";
 
lock = 0;
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'===============RequestSvTrEND ===============
 
' Function : Prepare the parameters for processing
 
' Call : By User
 
' Branch : None
 
' Argument : None
 
'
 
SUB RequestSvTrEND ()
 
IF (lock == 0) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;lock = 1;
 
&#160;&#160;' allocate the buffers
 
&#160;&#160;hNames = filetobuf (filesource);
 
&#160;&#160;hBranch = filetobuf (filesource);
 
&#160;&#160;hVariables = alloc_buffer (1024);
 
&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", hNames);
 
&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", hBranch);
 
&#160;&#160;EVENT ("ADDPROG", "RESULT.DONE", 1, "RESULTS.TRT", "", "compute");
 
&#160;&#160;@RESULT.DONE = 0;
 
&#160;&#160;processing ();
 
END IF
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'=============== Processing ===============
 
' Function : Prepare list of variables to process
 
' Call: Sub RequestSvTrend
 
' Branch : None
 
' Argument : None
 
'
 
SUB processing ()
 
DIM ch AS STR;
 
DIM i AS INTEGER;
 
DIM offset AS INTEGER;
 
DIM StartTime AS DOUBLE;
 
DIM EndTime AS DOUBLE;
 
DIM selection AS DOUBLE;
 
selection = 0;
 
StartTime= DATETIMEVALUE (@RESULT.START.DATE, @RESULT.START.TIME);
 
EndTime &#160;&#160;= DATETIMEVALUE (@RESULT.END.DATE, @RESULT.END.TIME);
 
i = 0;
 
offset = 0;
 
nb_variables = 0;
 
' composition of a buffer containing N variables
 
WHILE (i++ < MAX_VARIABLES)
 
&#160;&#160;ch = SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hNames, "\N", "STR");
 
&#160;&#160;IF (CMPSTRING (ch, "")) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;PUT_BUFFER (hVariables, offset, format ("%s,", ch));
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;offset = offset + LEN (ch) + 1;
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;nb_variables++;
 
&#160;&#160;ELSE
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;break;
 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
WEND
 
ch = SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hNames, "\N", "STR");
 
flagEnd = (CMPSTRING (ch, "") == 0);
 
ch = SEQ_BUFFER ("PREVFIELD", hNames, "\N", "STR");
 
' remove last separator
 
PUT_BUFFER (hVariables, offset, "");
 
SVTREND ("GETTREND", hVariables, "", StartDate, EndTime, selection, 2, hDest,
 
",,RESULT.DONE,");
 
END SUB
 
'
 
'=============== Compute ===============
 
'
 
' Function : Process the resulting buffer
 
' Call Event : RESULT.DONE
 
' Branch : None
 
' Argument : None
 
'
 
SUB compute ()
 
DIM ch AS STR;
 
DIM i AS INTEGER;
 
DIM hResult AS LONG;
 
DIM value AS SINGLE;
 
DIM BufLine AS LONG;
 
' Are there data?
 
IF (CMPSTRING (CGET_BUFFER (hDest, 0, 1), "")) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;hResult = SEQ_BUFFER ("CRTOCRLF", hDest, "NEW_BUFFER");
 
&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", hResult);
 
&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("SEEKFIELD", 1, hResult, ",");
 
&#160;&#160;FOR (i = 0; i < nb_variables; i++)
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;value = sval (SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hResult, ",", "STR"));
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;nbPoints [i] = 0; ' number of points found
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;SumValue [i] = 0; ' sum of values
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MinValue [i] = value; ' minimum of values
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MaxValue [i] = value; ' maximum of values
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;AveValue [i] = 0; ' mean
 
&#160;&#160;NEXT
 
&#160;&#160;BufLine = alloc_buffer (500);
 
&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", hResult);
 
&#160;&#160;WHILE (SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hResult, "\N", BufLine))
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;CalcStatistics (BufLine);
 
&#160;&#160;WEND
 
&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (hResult);
 
&#160;&#160;WHILE (svtrEND ("GETNEXTBUFFER", hDest))
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;hResult = SEQ_BUFFER ("CRTOCRLF", hDest, "NEW_BUFFER");
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", hResult);
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;WHILE (SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hResult, "\N", BufLine))
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;CalcStatistics (BufLine);
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;WEND
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (hResult);
 
&#160;&#160;WEND
 
&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (BufLine);
 
&#160;&#160;FOR (i = 0; i < nb_variables; i++)
 
&#160;&#160;' compute the mean
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;IF (nbPoints [i] != 0) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;AveValue [i] = SumValue [i] / nbPoints [i];
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;END IF
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' retrieve the variable name (branch)
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;ch = SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", hBranch, "\N", "STR");
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' assign values in database
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;?ch.MIN = MinValue [i];
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;?ch.MAX = MaxValue [i];
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;?ch.MOY = AveValue [i];
 
&#160;&#160;NEXT
 
END IF
 
&#160;
 
' processing ended?
 
IF (flagend) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;lock = 0;
 
&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (hNames);
 
&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (hBranch);
 
&#160;&#160;FREE_BUFFER (hVariables);
 
&#160;&#160;EVENT ("DEL", "RESULT.DONE", 1, "RESULTS.TRT", "", "compute");
 
&#160;&#160;' close the schedule WINDOW at start and end
 
&#160;&#160;WINDOW ("CLOSE", "RESULTS", "");
 
&#160;&#160;' open the window if mean temperatures, min and max
 
&#160;&#160;WINDOW ("OPEN", "MEANS", "");
 
ELSE
 
&#160;&#160;processing ();
 
END IF
 
END SUB
 
'
 
'=============== CalcStatistics===============
 
' Function : Compute the sum
 
' Call : Sub Process
 
' Branch : None
 
' Arg : Buffer containing the line to process
 
'
 
SUB CalcStatistics ( handle )
 
DIM ch AS STR;
 
DIM i AS INTEGER;
 
DIM flag AS INTEGER;
 
DIM value AS SINGLE;
 
i = 0;
 
flag = 1;
 
SEQ_BUFFER ("BEGIN", handle);
 
SEQ_BUFFER ("SEEKFIELD", 1, handle, ",");
 
WHILE (flag)
 
&#160;&#160;ch = SEQ_BUFFER ("NEXTFIELD", handle, ",", "STR");
 
&#160;&#160;flag = CMPSTRING (ch, "");
 
&#160;&#160;IF ((flag) && CMPSTRING (ch, "?")) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' number of valid points found
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;nbPoints [i]++;
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' retrieve the value at a point
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;value = sval (ch);
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' do the sum
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;SumValue [i] = SumValue [i] + value;
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' retrieve the min
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;IF (value < MinValue [i]) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MinValue [i] = value;
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;END IF
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;' retrieve the max
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;IF (value > MaxValue [i]) THEN
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;MaxValue [i] = value;
 
&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;END IF
 
&#160;&#160;END IF
 
&#160;&#160;i++;
 
WEND
 
END SUB

SYSTEM example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
SYSTEM Examples
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
Example 1 
 
This example exercises several modes of the SYSTEM command.
 
It requires creation of a User "user1" with 
 a password "pass1".
 
'Create a control variable of type State: @STATE01
 
'Mode SETDATE or Mode 1 (syntax 1)
 
&#160;
 
SUB systemsetdate()
 
'Declare variables
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
'change the system date 
 
intResult = SYSTEM("SETDATE", 28, 06, 2002);
 
END SUB 
 
&#160;
 
'Mode SETTIME or Mode 2 (syntax 2)
 
&#160;

'Declare variables

'change the time of the system

END SUB

Example 2
 
This example changes various system settings. 
 
Change system time forward by one hour:
 
SUB Summer ()
Const OneHour = 3600000;
DIM LongTime As Double;
DIM NewTime As Str;
DIM NewDate As Str;
LongTime = DateTimeValue () + OneHour;
NewDate = DateTimeString(LongTime, "D");
NewTime = DateTimeString(LongTime, "T");
SYSTEM(13, NewDate, NewTime);
END SUB
&#160;
 
Log a user onto the system:
 
SUB Boss ()
SYSTEM("LOGIN", "superuser", "bigboss");
END SUB
&#160;
 
SUB AutoOff ()
If (Activity == 0) Then
SYSTEM("LOGOUT");
End If
END SUB ()
 
Manage a multi-screen system: 
 
' START.PVB Loaded on startup of system
SUB Main ()
Program("PRELOAD", "setreg.pvb", "");
SYSTEM("SETSYSREGION", 2); ' two screens
END SUB
&#160;
 
' SETREG.PVB
SUB Main ()
END SUB
&#160;
 
SUB LeftWin (WinName)
SYSTEM("SETREGION", 1); ' Select left screen
WINDOW("OPEN", WinName);
END SUB
&#160;
 
SUB RightWin (WinName)
SYSTEM("SETREGION", 2); ' Select right screen
WINDOW("OPEN", WinName);
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: TEMPORARY_DB.htm
========================================
TEMPORARY_DB
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
TEMPORARY_DB
 

 
 

 

 

 See also 

Example
 
Activation, creation and deletion of temporary variables. 
 
WebVue support - Yes.
 
For further information, see the Variables Tree book in the
 Application Explorer Help.
 

 
Starting with version 12 and the introduction of HMI variables, temporary variables created with this instruction are obsolete. The TEMPORARY_DB instruction is only kept for compatibility with existing projects and should not be used in new projects. When migrating a project, we recommend you to replace the use of temporary variables by HMI variables. To make it easier, you can create HMI variables by script using the instruction VARIABLE.
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Syntax
 
 
 

 
 0 
 
 
 OFF 
 
 1
 
 
 

 
 1 
 
 
 ON 
 
 1
 
 
 

 
 2 
 
 
 ADDBIT 
 
 2
 
 
 

 
 3 
 
 
 ADDREG 
 
 3
 
 
 

 
 4 
 
 
 ADDTXT 
 
 4
 
 
 
 
Syntax 1
 
IntVal = TEMPORARY_DB(Mode);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
0
 
 
 
OFF
 
 
 
The automatic creation of temporary variables is disabled.
 
 
 

 
 
1
 
 
 
ON
 
 
 
The automatic creation of temporary variables is enabled.
 Any variables referred to in an animation that do not exist in the Variables Tree
 are automatically created at the HMI level, not in the Variables Tree.
 
 
 

 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
&#160;
 
 
 
Return: Always 1
 
 
 
 
Syntax 2
 
IntVal = TEMPORARY_DB(Mode, VarName,
 Title_1, Title_2);
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
VarName
 
 
 
The name for the temporary variable. Type STR
 
 
 

 
 
Title_1
 
 
 
The title for the temporary variable in the primary
 language. Type STR
 
 
 

 
 
Title_2
 
 
 
The title for the temporary variable in the alternative
 language. Type STR
 
 
 
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
2
 
 
 
ADDBIT
 
 
 
A temporary bit variable is created using the
 supplied attributes.
 
Return: 1 if successful,
else 0 (if the variable already exists, syntax error...).
 
 
 
 
Syntax 3
 
IntVal = TEMPORARY_DB(Mode, VarName,
 Title_1, Title_2,
 Min, Max, Format, Units);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
VarName
 
 
 
The name for the temporary variable. Type STR.
 
 
 

 
 
Title_1
 
 
 
The title for the temporary variable in the primary
 language. Type STR.
 
 
 

 
 
Min
 
 
 
The minimum value of the range of the register. Any numeric
 type.
 
 
 

 
 
Max
 
 
 
The maximum value of the range of the register. Any numeric
 type.
 
 
 

 
 
Format
 
 
 
The display format for the register, for example ###.##.
 Type STR.
 
 
 

 
 
Units
 
 
 
The unit text for the register, for example "DegC". Type
 STR.
 
 
 
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
3
 
 
 
ADDREG
 
 
 
A temporary register variable is created using
 the supplied attributes.
 
Return: 1 if successful,
else 0 if the variable already exists, syntax error etc.
 
 
 
 
Syntax 4
 
IntVal = TEMPORARY_DB(Mode, VarName,
 Title_1, Title_2);
 
Return type: INTEGER.
 
 
 
 

 
 
Argument
 
 
 
Meaning
 
 
 

 
 
VarName
 
 
 
The name for the temporary variable. Type STR
 
 
 

 
 
Title_1
 
 
 
The title for the temporary variable in the primary
 language. Type STR
 
 
 

 
 
Title_2
 
 
 
The title for the temporary variable in the alternative
 language. Type STR
 
 
 
 
Execution
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Mode
 
 
 
Mnemonic
 
 
 
Action
 
 
 

 
 
4
 
 
 
ADDTXT
 
 
 
A temporary text variable is created using the
 supplied attributes.
 
Return: 1 if successful,
else 0 if the variable already exists, syntax error etc.
 
 
 
 
Example
 
DIM ConstVarName As Str; 
DIM VarName As Str; 
DIM Index As Integer; 
ConstVarName = "TempBit" 
 
'Create 100 bit variables: TempBit0 to TempBit99 
For(Index = 0; Index < 100; Index ++) 
VarName = AddString(ConstVarName, TOC(Index)); 
Temporary_DB("ADDBIT", VarName, "", "");
Next
 
For a further example, select the Example link above.

TOPIC: TEMPORARY_DB_Example.htm

TEMPORARY_DB example
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
TEMPORARY_DB Example
 

 
 

 

 

 Applies to
 
 
This example exercises several modes of the TEMPORARY_DB 
 command.
 
'Mode OFF or Mode 0 (syntax 1)
 
&#160;
 
SUB temporarydboff()
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("OFF");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'Mode ON or Mode 1 (syntax 1)
 
&#160;
 
SUB temporarydbon()
 
'enables creation of temporary variables in animations and symbols.
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("ON");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'Mode ADDBIT or Mode 2 (syntax 2)
 
&#160;
 
SUB temporarydbaddbit()
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("ADDBIT", "@STATEAB03", "label� language 1", "label� language 2");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'Mode ADDREG or Mode 3 (syntax 3)
 
&#160;
 
SUB temporarydbaddreg()
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("ADDREG", "@REGISTER01", "label� language 1", "label� language 2", -20, 35, "##.##u", "�C");
 
END SUB
 
&#160;
 
'Mode ADDTXT or Mode 4 (syntax 4)
 
&#160;
 
SUB temporarydbaddtxt()
 
DIM intResult as integer;
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("ADDTXT", "@TEXTE01", "label� language 1", "label� language 2", 32);
 
intResult = TEMPORARY_DB("ADDTXT", "@TEXTE02", "label� language 1", "label� language 2", 32);
 
END SUB


========================================
TOPIC: Temporary_Variables.htm
========================================
Temporary Variables
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Temporary Variables
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 See also
 
 
Temporary variables may be created by using the instruction 
 TEMPORARY_DB. They are visible to both the program language and the HMI. 
 Temporary variables use far less memory than conventional variables. 
 They are ideal for use in large projects where a program is calculating 
 a variable that will only be used for purposes of animation. 
 
For more information on temporary variables, see the book 
 on The Variables Tree and the topic on the instruction TEMPORARY_DB.
